justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and two of six vehicles, missing most objects and therefore incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes for detected entities such as motion status, door/trunk state, type, age, and communicative/transporting labels, despite missing many objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several useful spatial relations (e.g., left of truck, center-left, ahead), but relations are sparse and omit many scene objects and relations.
justifications.overall: Extraction offers reasonably detailed attributes for a few correctly identified objects but is overall limited by low recall of pedestrians and vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and two vehicles but missed two other vehicles present in the ground truth and omitted some per-vehicle motion annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides rich attributes (scene description, pedestrian actions, age, communicative/transporting fields) for detected entities but fails to correctly capture some crucial semantics like vehicle motion for the black van and lacks entries for missing vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the reported objects appear plausible (eg, truck occupying center lane, van behind truck) but are incomplete because two vehicles are missing and one vehicle has an incorrect motion status that affects relational interpretation.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: good descriptive detail for detected objects but incomplete object coverage and some incorrect labels (notably vehicle motion) limit overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and two of five vehicles, missing several objects and thus covering roughly 43% of annotated entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes (actions, age, vehicle types, door/trunk status) for detected objects but omits many entities and some ground-truth fields, giving moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations supplied for the reported objects are plausible and helpful (curb, near truck, ahead/center) but relations are absent for most missing vehicles, so coverage is partial.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is moderate: attribute accuracy for detected objects is decent but significant omissions in object detection and relations limit overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 9 annotated objects and missed multiple pedestrians and parked vehicles, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many semantic attributes such as actions, age, transport and door/trunk states, but mislabels one pedestrian action and omits attributes for several missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides explicit spatial relations for detected agents that are mostly reasonable but contains inaccuracies and does not cover many objects present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance due to decent semantic descriptions but low recall of objects and some action and spatial errors.

justifications.completeness: Detected all three pedestrians but misclassified two atomic actions and only detected 3 of 5 ground-truth vehicles, missing several objects and motion labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attributes and contextual details (track hints, spatial relations, object types, door/trunk states) beyond the ground truth, though some attributes are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are plausible and useful (sidewalk, next to/behind, ahead of ego), but some relations are imprecise or inconsistent with the true motion/parking status and multiple objects lack detailed relations.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: good descriptive richness and pedestrian coverage but notable errors in pedestrian actions, incomplete vehicle detection, and some spatial/motion inaccuracies.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 4 pedestrians and 4 of 6 vehicles (roughly 58% coverage), missing two pedestrians and two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-object attributes (age, motion_status, trunk/doors, and descriptive types) but misses or mislabels important person attributes like carrying and jaywalking and misclassifies a walking pedestrian as standing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes detailed local relations between people and vehicles that are mostly plausible, but omits relations for the missing objects and fails to capture contextual relations present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance with reasonable attribute richness and spatial descriptions but incomplete detection and some incorrect person labels reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 6 ground-truth pedestrians and 5 of 7 ground-truth vehicles, missing multiple objects and thus providing incomplete coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures core attributes like walking and basic motion status but misses important labels (jaywalking, carrying with both hands, talking on phone) and misattributes or omits some vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides numerous relative positions that are generally useful, but some relations are vague or fail to mark critical contextual cues (e.g., location of a jaywalking pedestrian).
justifications.overall: Partial but useful extraction: accurate for several detected entities but overall limited by missed objects and key semantic mismatches.

justifications.completeness: Low completeness: the extraction reports 4 pedestrians and 3 vehicles while the ground truth contains 7 persons and 5 vehicles and it also omits at least one transporting label.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate semantic richness: many attribute types are provided (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several labels are misassigned or missing compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Moderate spatial relation quality: some relations like the central black van and parked vehicles are captured, but relations are incomplete and a few are imprecise or inconsistent with the annotations.
justifications.overall: Average overall: the extraction conveys useful information but is notably incomplete and contains label assignment errors that reduce its reliability for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses 2 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles from the ground truth and omits several per-agent attributes, so overall coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: While some attributes (actions, communicative states) are provided, several important semantics such as carrying and group-talking are missing or possibly misassigned, reducing fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: The VLM provides many plausible spatial relations (e.g., parked left, ahead of ego, inside garage) but some relations are ambiguous or not clearly aligned to ground-truth object identities.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially informative with useful spatial cues but misses multiple actors and key semantic attributes, making it insufficient for reliable decision making.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and 3 of 4 vehicles while omitting several persons and failing to report some GT attributes such as transporting, so coverage is moderate-low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies many semantic labels (actions, context, communicative cues, and descriptive spatial details) but it misses or misattributes key ground-truth fields (e.g., a GT transporting label and some communicative labels) and omits several actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations are detailed and plausible for reported actors, but several actors are missing and some relations may be misaligned with the ground truth, reducing overall spatial coverage and reliability.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially useful with reasonable semantic detail for reported objects but is substantially incomplete and contains attribute omissions and some misassignments, yielding a middling overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing several pedestrians and one moving vehicle, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (action, age, some spatial relations) but omits important labels from ground truth (jaywalking, carrying with both hands, communicative behaviors) and many attributes are left as 'none of the above'.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations provided for detected agents are generally accurate (e.g., crossing in front, adjacent to parked van) but many actors lack any spatial relation, reducing overall spatial completeness.
justifications.overall: Partial but useful extraction with reasonable spatial descriptions for detected objects, however it misses multiple actors and several critical semantic labels, yielding a below-average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 4 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing key actors and attributes such as carrying and communicative labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic action and location labels but omits several important attributes present in ground truth (jaywalking, carrying, talking in group) and identifies fewer vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are reasonably described for detected entities (left curb, right sidewalk, center ahead) but several actors lack spatial tags and some contextual relations like illegal crossing are missing.
justifications.overall: Partial but incomplete extraction capturing some spatial layout and motions but missing multiple actors and key semantic attributes, yielding limited utility.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected only 3 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles from the ground truth, missing key instances such as the person talking on the phone and several vehicles, so coverage is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: For detected agents the extraction provides multiple attributes (actions, context, age, transport and door/trunk states), but it fails to capture the communicative label present in ground truth and omits many objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial relations given are generally plausible and useful (e.g., parked on left, ahead of ego), but they are not provided for all ground truth objects and thus are incomplete.
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately informative for the objects it finds, offering useful semantic details and plausible spatial cues, but its overall utility is limited by missing object detections and some missed labels.

justifications.completeness: Missing one annotated pedestrian and several vehicles from the ground truth so the extraction covers only a small portion of the scene objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction reports many per-agent attributes such as atomic action, simple context, age, and vehicle door/trunk states, but it misses a communicative label and several object instances, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are useful for the reported agents and vehicles, but relations are absent for missing objects and some relations are coarse or possibly inaccurate.
justifications.overall: While attribute-level detail is reasonable for reported entities, low object coverage and some incorrect or coarse context reduce the overall quality to below average.

justifications.completeness: Captured 4 of 6 pedestrians and 3 of 5 vehicles (missing two pedestrians including one 'talking in group' and one 'jaywalking', and a stopped vehicle), so several objects and critical labels are absent.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-object attributes (age, motion_status, trunk/doors, simple context) but fails to recover some important semantic labels such as the communicative 'talking in group' and the 'jaywalking' simple context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible relative locations for extracted entities (e.g., parked along left curb, center ahead) but is incomplete due to missing objects and lacks verification against some ground-truth relations.
justifications.overall: Moderately accurate with useful attribute coverage and spatial cues, but incomplete object detection and missed critical semantic labels reduce overall utility for driving safety tasks.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected the four pedestrians but misclassified one as a child and missed several vehicle instances and some vehicle motion states present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains many attributes (actions, contexts, age, carrying, doors/trunk) and relational details, but it missed key context labels (jaywalking, talking in group) and misassigned at least one age and some vehicle motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for most entities and are broadly useful (left/right/ahead/on curb), however some relations lack precision relative to the ground truth and moving vehicles are not fully localized in relation to the ego.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is moderately useful due to rich semantic details but reduced reliability from missing vehicle instances, motion mislabels, and a notable age/context error.

justifications.completeness: Detected all four pedestrians but only three of five vehicles and missed several object-level labels such as a stopped vehicle and nuanced contextual tags.
justifications.semantic_richness: Outputs many attribute fields (actions, context, doors/trunk) but fails to capture important semantics present in the ground truth like jaywalking and group communication.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides detailed spatial relations for most objects that broadly match the scene, though a few positional relations appear imprecise or unchecked against ground truth tracks.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: reasonable pedestrian coverage and spatial descriptions but missing vehicles and key semantic nuances limit reliability for safety-critical understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses two pedestrians from the ground truth and fails to report the moving vehicles present, despite capturing several parked/stopped vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies many semantic attributes (actions, communicative cues, object types, trunk/door states) but omits or mislabels nuanced context labels such as jaywalking and 'talking in group'.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several useful spatial relations are provided (near, parked on curb, in front of ego) but critical positional context for jaywalking pedestrians and motion-related spatial cues for moving vehicles are missing or ambiguous.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction offers decent semantic detail and spatial hints but is undermined by missed pedestrians, missed moving vehicles, and some contextual mislabeling limiting reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses two ground-truth pedestrians and one vehicle, and omits communicative/jaywalking labels present in the ground truth, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many attributes (actions, contexts, age, trunk/doors, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth but also fails to capture or mislabels key communicative/jaywalking semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes detailed spatial relations for most reported entities, but some relations contradict ground-truth contextual cues (e.g., jaywalkers not identified) and one vehicle is missing.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate and semantically rich extraction but incomplete and with notable omissions/mislabels, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 4 pedestrians and 3 of 4 vehicles, missing at least one pedestrian and a stopped vehicle, so partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (age, motion, doors) but fails to label 'jaywalking' and mislabels a sidewalk walker as 'standing', reducing semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides coarse spatial cues (center, left sidewalk, ahead) for most objects but omits the stopped vehicle and lacks precise 'jaywalking' location, limiting spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Moderately accurate extraction with useful attributes but notable omissions and key context errors (missed 'jaywalking' labels and a missing stopped vehicle) lower overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing one pedestrian and at least two moving vehicles while misreporting vehicle motion status.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (age, actions, communicative status, transporting, scene description) and object-level details beyond the ground truth, though a few key attributes like vehicle motion are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Supplies useful spatial relations for most detected agents but omits one pedestrian and includes some relations that are vague or affected by incorrect motion labeling.
justifications.overall: Moderate quality extraction with strong semantic detail but notable omissions and incorrect motion/matching reduce its reliability for safety-critical perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected most pedestrians and two vehicles but omitted a moving vehicle, included an extra pedestrian, and failed to mark two pedestrians as jaywalking.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (actions, age, transporting, trunk/door states, spatial relations) and contextual hints but misses the critical jaywalking labels and misattributes one vehicle type/motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and generally accurate for the reported agents (ahead, center of lane, near storefront) but one moving vehicle is not reported so its relations are absent and some relations are somewhat vague.
justifications.overall: Good attribute coverage and useful spatial cues but reduced overall reliability due to missed/misclassified agents and failure to capture safety-critical jaywalking context.

justifications.completeness: Misses one pedestrian and two moving vehicles from the ground truth and mislabels a moving vehicle as stopped.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (actions, age, communicative, transporting, doors/trunk) and a useful scene description but omits important labels like jaywalking and incorrectly reports vehicle motion for some objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several correct local relations (ahead/on sidewalk/near storefront) but some relations are imprecise or tied to a misclassified vehicle and relations for omitted objects are missing.
justifications.overall: Decent attribute coverage and scene context, but incomplete detection and some incorrect motion/context labels reduce overall reliability for driving decisions.

justifications.completeness: Detected four pedestrians and two vehicles matching most ground-truth objects but missed one moving vehicle and failed to mark jaywalking context for two pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attributes (actions, age, communicative, transporting, spatial relations) but missed key semantic labels from the ground truth (jaywalking) and misclassified one pedestrian's simple_context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Most spatial relations are appropriate (van ahead, bicycle on left curb, pedestrian positions) though some relations are coarse and not tied to ground-truth IDs.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and descriptive detail but omissions (one moving vehicle) and missed jaywalking labels reduce overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies 3 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 3 ground-truth vehicles, so several objects are missing despite correct detections for some.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output supplies many attributes (actions, context, age, transport, motion status, door/trunk states, types) beyond the GT fields, showing high attribute coverage despite occasional context mismatches.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are plausible and some match the GT (e.g., van ahead, parked bike at curb), but several pedestrian locations are imprecise or omitted reducing spatial accuracy and completeness.
justifications.overall: Good semantic detail and several correct detections, but missing multiple pedestrians and one vehicle plus some spatial imprecision yields a moderate overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 pedestrians (missing two) while all three vehicles are listed, so entity coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Many semantic attributes are provided for pedestrians and vehicles, but two moving vehicles are mislabeled as stopped and some trunk/door states are asserted without ground-truth support.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and generally consistent with the scene (truck ahead, van behind, bicycle left), though a few relations are ambiguous and not directly verifiable against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is useful and semantically rich but reduced by missing pedestrians and incorrect vehicle motion classifications.

justifications.completeness: Detected most ground truth pedestrians and both ground truth vehicles but missed one person.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides rich attributes for actors and vehicles but contains at least one incorrect action label and some unsupported inferences.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes many spatial relations and relative ordering for vehicles but has errors in pedestrian spatial/context labels and unverifiable directional claims.
justifications.overall: Overall reasonably complete and informative with accurate vehicle reporting but some pedestrian omissions and spatial/action errors lower reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected most pedestrians and one vehicle but missed one pedestrian and one vehicle and misclassified one pedestrian's action.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a broad set of attributes (actions, contexts, age, vehicle states) that align well with ground truth despite some attribute errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes meaningful spatial relations for detected entities, but missing objects and a misclassified pedestrian reduce accuracy.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: good semantic detail but incomplete object coverage and some attribute errors lead to a middling overall score.

justifications.completeness: The VLM found most pedestrians and the vehicle but missed at least one person (the standing subject) and did not label the jaywalking instance, so detections are incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attributes (actions, contexts, age, transporting, and vehicle state) and added contextual details, though it introduced an incorrect transporting label and missed one action class.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations and relative placements are generally provided and largely plausible, but some positional/contextual behaviors like jaywalking are omitted and one pedestrian is unaccounted for.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is moderately good with useful semantic detail and mostly correct spatial info, but it is weakened by a missed person, a missing jaywalking label, and one erroneous transporting inference.

justifications.completeness: Detected 5 of 6 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles from the ground truth, so detection is mostly complete but missing one person and one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction reports many attributes (atomic action, context, age, communicative, transporting) for detections, but several simple_context labels disagree with ground truth and one pedestrian is missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for most agents and the van, but some relations appear imprecise or inconsistent with ground truth and the second vehicle is omitted, reducing spatial accuracy and completeness.
justifications.overall: Good overall coverage and attribute detail, but performance is reduced by one missing pedestrian, a missing vehicle, and several incorrect simple_context and spatial relation assignments.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 8 ground-truth pedestrians and the single ground-truth vehicle, but missed multiple pedestrians and added an unannotated parked scooter.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (atomic actions, simple context, communicative, transporting, age, and vehicle states) offering richer semantics than the ground truth, though some context labels are inaccurate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for detected agents and are generally reasonable, but several pedestrians' precise contexts (e.g., jaywalking vs sidewalk) were missed and some agents are omitted.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures useful semantics and correct relations for detected objects but is incomplete in detection coverage and has contextual labeling errors, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses one pedestrian (the bicyclist/sitting) and at least two vehicles including a stopped vehicle, so the extraction is substantially incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like age and motion for vehicles and pedestrians but omits important context labels such as biking and jaywalking and fails to annotate several object instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible relative placements (parked on left curb, near centerline ahead) for detected objects and these appear consistent with the walking pedestrian, though spatial coverage is limited by missing detections.
justifications.overall: Partial correctness with reasonable spatial descriptions for reported items but low completeness and missing critical semantic labels lead to a below average overall evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and two vehicles but missed multiple annotated vehicles and failed to capture one pedestrian's correct action and context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, age, doors, trunk) but omits key context labels such as jaywalking and biking and mislabels one pedestrian action.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the reported agents are precise and relevant, though relations for the missing vehicles and stopped vehicle are absent.
justifications.overall: Reasonably detailed for the objects it reports, but omissions of several vehicles and an incorrect pedestrian action reduce overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Misses one person who is sitting and biking, omits at least one parked vehicle from the ground truth, and mislabels another vehicle's motion, so only partial coverage of annotated entities and their key states.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many descriptive attributes (age, atomic_action, trunk_open, doors_open, spatial notes) offering rich semantics, but some attributes are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth which reduces trustworthiness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations like parked along left curb and in middle of road, but omits critical contextual labels such as jaywalking and contains some positional/motion inaccuracies.
justifications.overall: Moderately useful extraction with substantial semantic detail and spatial cues, but notable omissions and incorrect labels for key actors and motions lower the overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Misses one parked vehicle from the ground truth and mislabels the first pedestrian (ground truth indicates biking/sitting while the extraction says pushing/walking), although the jaywalking pedestrian and other vehicles are reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (age, transporting, trunk/door state, communicative) and richer contextual descriptions than the ground truth, but includes incorrect labels for the first pedestrian and asserts some vehicle attributes where GT is unknown.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the reported objects (left, ahead, between pedestrians and ego) are reasonable and useful, but one vehicle is omitted and some GT spatial detail is not available to fully verify accuracy.
justifications.overall: A moderately useful extraction that captures several correct entities and rich semantics but suffers from a critical pedestrian misclassification and at least one missing vehicle, reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians and one vehicle but missed the parked vehicle and mislabelled some atomic actions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many semantic attributes (age, atomic action, transporting, trunk/doors) beyond the ground truth, though some action labels are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations for the reported agents are mostly accurate (left/right/ahead) but the model invented details (potted plants) and omitted the parked vehicle's relation.
justifications.overall: Good coverage of scene elements and rich attributes but errors in atomic actions and a missed vehicle reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies the two people but misses several annotated vehicles (including a moving vehicle) and mislabels or omits some object-level attributes, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle doors/trunk) and descriptive context, but some atomic actions and vehicle states conflict with ground truth and several fields are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are detailed and plausible for the reported objects, but they do not cover all ground-truth vehicles and some spatial claims may be inconsistent with missing/mislabeled objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful semantic detail for detected entities but misses multiple ground-truth vehicles and has some incorrect or incomplete attribute assignments, yielding a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but missed most vehicles and misidentified several atomic actions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a wide set of semantic attributes for each agent but several key attributes are incorrect or ambiguous.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible relative positions for detected agents which are generally reasonable though not verified against ground truth coordinates.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance with good pedestrian coverage and attribute variety, but significant errors in actions and missing vehicles reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple ground truth vehicles and mislabels the pedestrian action and a moving vehicle as stopped, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, motion, doors, trunk, communicative) beyond ground truth, but some attributes are incorrect or set to none, giving moderate richness.

justifications.completeness: Captured both pedestrians but missed several vehicles from the ground truth and misclassified the moving van as parked.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations that largely match scene geometry (curbs, ahead of ego) though some motion-related spatial inferences are incorrect for the van.

justifications.completeness: Detected the bicyclist but missed multiple vehicles including the moving vehicle and underreported objects, so coverage is low.

justifications.completeness: Misses most annotated vehicles and mislabels or omits several parked vehicles, though it captures the single person.

justifications.completeness: Recognizes the cyclist/person and a few vehicle instances but misses several vehicles present in the ground truth and omits some vehicle attributes.

justifications.overall: Moderately useful extraction with good semantic detail for detected objects but reduced reliability due to missed vehicle detections and some inaccurate attributes.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only one person and two vehicle-like objects while the ground truth contains multiple vehicles and more detailed annotations, so many entities and attributes are missing or unaligned.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only six pedestrians (ground truth has eight) and omits all three annotated vehicles as well as multiple transporting labels, so many expected entities are missing.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 6 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, missing multiple persons and one vehicle so coverage is partial.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds 4 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 3 ground-truth vehicles, so a substantial fraction of objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: All annotated entities are present and the vehicle is detected, but key attributes are missing or misassigned for at least one person.

justifications.spatial_relations: Relative positions and proximity are described accurately and align well with the ground truth scene layout.
justifications.overall: Good detection and spatial description with correct vehicle motion, but notable errors in action and age annotations reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses the vehicle, mislabels ages for both people, and omits or incorrectly assigns several atomic actions so many ground-truth fields are absent or wrong.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple descriptive attributes and contexts (scene description, actions, transport, relations) but contains factual errors (age and some actions) reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible spatial relations that correspond to the scene layout, though ground truth lacks explicit relations for full verification.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic coverage but key errors like missing vehicle and incorrect ages/actions lower overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Included both the person and vehicle but missed the person's atomic action (sitting), misclassified age, and added unsupported trunk/door states.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides rich descriptive context and multiple attributes (scene, activities, relations) but uses a coarse age label and fabricates trunk/door information.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives accurate and detailed spatial relations for the cyclist and bicycle that align with the scene description.
justifications.overall: Reasonably detailed and spatially accurate extraction, but incorrect atomic action, age labeling, and invented vehicle door/trunk states reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Correctly detects the single person and most attributes (action, contexts, absence of vehicles) but fails to match the ground truth age label.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic attributes and a scene description, but the incorrect age classification reduces semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately describes the person's pose and location relative to the curb and wall, aligning well with the scene.
justifications.overall: Good detection and rich labels with accurate spatial relations, but the incorrect age label lowers the overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies both pedestrians and the bicycle and supplies most fields, but it mislabels both ages and omits the bicyclist's atomic action compared to the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes many semantic attributes and detailed spatial relations beyond the ground truth, though some semantic labels (age, one atomic action) are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported spatial relations (bicyclist on the right near the curb and in front of the seated person) are detailed and align well with the scene.
justifications.overall: Strong coverage and rich relations make the extraction useful but errors in age classification and one atomic action meaningfully reduce accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Captured the person, action, and contextual attributes but missed the specific age label and an explicit track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes and spatial details but uses a generic age label instead of the ground truth senior classification.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are accurate and match the scene description, correctly locating the person on the right curb against the wall.
justifications.overall: Good extraction of action and location but the incorrect age classification and missing explicit track id reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: All required person attributes are present and vehicles are correctly empty, but the age is incorrectly labeled as adult instead of senior over 65.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes action, context, communicative and spatial relations providing good semantic detail but misses correct age granularity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations accurately describe the person as on the right curb and next to the wall, matching the scene description.
justifications.overall: Strong extraction for action and location with good detail overall, but the incorrect age classification reduces reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the single pedestrian and many fields but omits an explicit track id, omits complex context, and contains incorrect age and communicative labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides extra descriptive details such as clothing and multiple spatial relations beyond the ground truth, but some semantic attributes are incorrect which reduces trust.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported spatial relations accurately reflect the ground truth positioning of the person sitting on the right curb against the wall.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and rich spatial description are offset by incorrect estimations for age and communicative activity and the missing track identifier, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected the person, action, and contextual labels but failed to provide the more specific age annotation (senior over 65) and an explicit track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes action, simple context, and spatial detail but uses a generic age label instead of the ground truth senior over 65 and offers no additional complex context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are accurate and match the scene (sitting on right curb adjacent to wall).
justifications.overall: Accurate on action and location but overall quality is reduced by the incorrect/insufficient age classification.

justifications.completeness: Covers the single person and most annotated attributes including action and context but omits the explicit object track id and mislabels age.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic attributes (action, context, spatial relations) but lacks the correct age category and the object identifier, reducing attribute fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are accurate and match the scene description, correctly placing the person on the right next to the wall.
justifications.overall: High-quality extraction with accurate action and spatial cues but penalized for the incorrect age classification and missing track id.
